2604.18563.title: Dual Alignment Between Language Model Layers and Human Sentence Processing
2604.18563.paper_summary: A recent study (Kuribayashi et al., 2025) has shown that human sentence processing behavior, typically measured on syntactically unchallenging constructions, can be effectively modeled using surprisal from early layers of large language models (LLMs). This raises the question of whether such advantages of internal layers extend to more syntactically challenging constructions, where surprisal has been reported to underestimate human cognitive effort. In this paper, we begin by exploring internal layers that better estimate human cognitive effort observed in syntactic ambiguity processing in English. Our experiments show that, in contrast to naturalistic reading, later layers better estimate such a cognitive effort, but still underestimate the human data. This dual alignment sheds light on different modes of sentence processing in humans and LMs: naturalistic reading employs a somewhat weak prediction akin to earlier layers of LMs, while syntactically challenging processing requires more fully-contextualized representations, better modeled by later layers of LMs. Motivated by these findings, we also explore several probability-update measures using shallow and deep layers of LMs, showing a complementary advantage to single-layer's surprisal in reading time modeling.
2604.18563.paper_summary_zh: 最近的一項研究（Kuribayashi et al., 2025）顯示，人類的句子處理行為，通常在語法上不具挑戰性的結構中進行測量，可以有效地使用大型語言模型（LLMs）早期層的驚訝度進行建模。<lb/>這引發了一個問題，即這些內部層的優勢是否擴展到更具語法挑戰性的結構中，因為在這些結構中，驚訝度被報導為低估了人類的認知努力。<lb/>在本文中，我們首先探討那些更好地估計在英語語法歧義處理中觀察到的人類認知努力的內部層。<lb/>我們的實驗顯示，與自然閱讀相比，後期層更好地估計這種認知努力，但仍然低估了人類數據。<lb/>這種雙重對齊揭示了人類和LLMs中不同的句子處理模式：自然閱讀使用類似於LLMs早期層的較弱預測，而語法挑戰性的處理則需要更完全上下文化的表徵，更好地由LLMs的後期層建模。<lb/>受到這些發現的啟發，我們還探討了使用LLMs的淺層和深層的幾個概率更新措施，顯示出在閱讀時間建模中，單層的驚訝度具有互補優勢。
2604.18563.author: Tatsuki Kuribayashi et.al.
2604.18563.authors: Tatsuki Kuribayashi, Alex Warstadt, Yohei Oseki, Ethan Gotlieb Wilcox
